commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Workboard edit-modal comment display, a comment draft field that calls `workboard.cards.comment`, and focused view-test coverage.

PR surface: Source +83, Tests +35. Total +118 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the comments UI slice from source inspection: current main has Workboard comment metadata, badges, search text, and a gateway comment method, but the edit modal does not render comments or an append control. The broader linked drag/status and save/reopen symptoms are not reproduced by this PR review.

target.title: fix(ui): show Workboard comments in edit modal
target.body:
## Summary

- Show existing Workboard card comments in the edit modal.
- Add a small comment draft field that calls the existing `workboard.cards.comment` gateway method.
- Keep the patch scoped to the existing Workboard UI controller/view path, with no new i18n keys or generated locale churn.

Refs #88592.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/views/workboard.test.ts`
- `node --import tsx scripts/control-ui-i18n.ts check`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:ui`
- `git diff --check`
- `node --import tsx .artifacts/control-ui-e2e/workboard-comments/proof.ts`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Opening a Workboard card edit modal now exposes existing comments and lets an operator add a comment from the same modal.
Real environment tested: Local Vite Control UI served by `startControlUiE2eServer`, mocked Gateway WebSocket via `installMockGateway`, and Google Chrome driven by Playwright.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx .artifacts/control-ui-e2e/workboard-comments/proof.ts`; the proof opened `/workboard`, loaded a mocked card with one existing comment, opened the edit modal, verified the existing comment was visible, filled a new comment, clicked `Create`, asserted the `workboard.cards.comment` request body, and verified the modal rendered the newly returned comment.
Evidence after fix: The browser proof captured local ignored artifacts: `.artifacts/control-ui-e2e/workboard-comments/01-workboard-card-with-comment-badge.png`, `02-edit-modal-existing-comment.png`, `03-edit-modal-comment-draft.png`, `04-edit-modal-comment-added.png`, and a `.webm` recording. The generated `summary.json` records the observed gateway call as `{ "id": "card-1", "body": "Ship after CI" }`.
Observed result after fix: Chrome showed the edit modal with `Needs owner check`; after the gateway comment call returned, the same modal showed both `Needs owner check` and `Ship after CI`.
What was not tested: Live Gateway/provider data, drag/status persistence, and the broader save/reopen symptoms from #88592 remain out of scope for this narrow comments UI PR.
target.author: monkeyleet
